headlineSummary: Anthropic drew the most negative discussion on limited evidence, while OpenAI and Google Gemini were both mildly positive and in a close tie [E1][E2][E3].

ranking.summary: Anthropic was described negatively for bloated Claude Code output and broader reliability concerns, with limited evidence behind that lean [E1][E2].
Google Gemini had a mildly positive signal from a comment saying Google models especially performed well on C# problem solving, and it was in a close tie with OpenAI [E3].
OpenAI had a mildly positive comment noting ChatGPT one-shotted a PHP app, and it was in a close tie with Google Gemini [E1].

evidence.excerpt: Today, Claude Code, the major hotness, made changes to a (different) toy app with less complexity, in node.js and you know what, it has ~49,000 files. And uses a 3rd party SaaS to host the db, and another 3rd party SaaS to do auth, and yet another 3rd party SaaS to import a repo and do deployments and hosting. Simplicity has a place for some things. I love PHP+SQLite for tiny apps, and LLMs/agents are awesome at it. I forgot, ChatGPT one-shotted the PHP app and Cursor put the finishing touches on. What a world.

annotations.rationale: The commenter contrasts Claude Code unfavorably with a simpler app, criticizing it for producing a bloated result with many files and SaaS dependencies.
The commenter says ChatGPT "one-shotted" the PHP app, indicating it performed the task successfully and efficiently.

evidence.excerpt: This is too weird for me. At least with programming languages I can consult the documentation and if the programming language isn’t behaving as documented, it’s obviously a defect and if you’re savvy enough you often have open channels that accept contributions. Can we say the same for Claude or other AI solutions?

annotations.rationale: The commenter expresses skepticism about Claude's reliability and accountability compared with conventional programming languages, implying lower trust.

evidence.excerpt: C# is one of the best languages we evaluated for LLM problem solving. In fact, many strongly typed, lower level languages perform well in our testing (this isn't to say that the solutions are more maintainable, but the LLMs come up with better ideas when writing C# or Rust than Python). Google models especially, for whatever reason, outperform in C#, not Golang.

annotations.rationale: In clear AI-model context, the commenter says "Google models especially" outperform for this coding task setting, which is favorable evidence for Google's tracked model family.
